title: podman machine ssh failed on windows
body:
### Issue Description

`podman machine ssh` failed on windows due to error identity file path

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a `podman-machine-default` with podman desktop on windows, using wsl2 (not hyper-v)
2. Run `podman machine ssh`
3. Conntion fails.


### Describe the results you received

> PS C:\Users\zgblc> podman machine ssh
> Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
> Warning: Identity file C:Userszgblc.localsharecontainerspodmanmachinemachine not accessible: No such file or directory.
> root@localhost's password:

### Describe the results you expected

The ssh key file exists `C:\Users\zgblc\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\machine`, but the code of `podman machine ssh` does not use manipulate the path correctly.

`path/filepath` from go std should be used to handle it correctly.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

podman machine runs in wsl2 distrubution `podman-machine-default`

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: My fault, I redirect windows's ssh to wsl for `ControlMaster` utility, so when podman passes windows identity file path, it was the linux ssh in wsl acturally received it, so it traits `\` as excape character and generate that error message.
